results.title: بعد ثنائيته أمام السويد.. مبابي يقترب من رقم ميسي التاريخي في كأس العالم
results.description:
واصل النجم الفرنسي كيليان مبابي كتابة التاريخ في كأس العالم، بعدما سجل هدفين في فوز فرنسا على السويد صباح الثلاثاء ضمن دور الـ32 من مونديال 2026، ليرفع رصيده إلى 18 هدفًا في تاريخ مشاركاته بالبطولة.

وبات مبابي على بعد هدف واحد فقط من معادلة الرقم القياسي المسجل باسم الأرجنتيني ليونيل ميسي، الهداف التاريخي لكأس العالم برصيد 19 هدفًا سجلها في 29 مباراة منذ نسخة 2006.

ويتميز مهاجم فرنسا بفعالية تهديفية لافتة، إذ وصل إلى 18 هدفًا خلال 18 مباراة فقط في النهائيات منذ ظهوره الأول في نسخة 2018، متفوقًا على العديد من أساطير اللعبة من حيث معدل التسجيل.

ويحتل الألماني ميروسلاف كلوزه المركز الثالث في قائمة الهدافين التاريخيين للمونديال برصيد 16 هدفًا، يليه البرازيلي رونالدو (15)، ثم الألماني غيرد مولر (14)، فيما يأتي الفرنسي جوست فونتين سادسًا برصيد 13 هدفًا.

وضمن اللاعبين الحاليين، يتساوى الإنجليزي هاري كاين والبرتغالي كريستيانو رونالدو في القائمة برصيد 11 و10 أهداف على التوالي، بينما يواصل مبابي مطاردة الصدارة التاريخية مع استمرار مشوار المنتخب الفرنسي في النسخة الحالية من كأس العالم.

results.title: Yunshan CorePark: From "Lingnan Everyday Life" to "International Daily"
results.description: KINGBUY Commercial Builds a 465,000 sq m Headquarters Project to Create "the Lingnan Living Room Closest to the World" GUANGZHOU, China , July 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- KINGBUY Commercial announced that its Guangzhou's Baiyun New Town headquarters project has been officially named Yunshan CorePark. Positioned as "the Lingnan living room closest to the world," the project represents a total investment of over RMB 3 billion, with a gross floor area of 465,000 sq m and plans to host more than 600 brands. It is Guangzhou's largest "LIFE MALL" to date. Leveraging key transportation hubs including Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport and Guangzhou Baiyun Railway Station, the project is designed to serve international exchanges and high-end business consumption, filling the gap for premium commercial facilities in Baiyun New Town and supporting Guangzhou's goal of becoming an international consumption center city. Aerial View The project integrates diverse functions to create a one-stop international living destination. With culture, relaxation, human touch, and community at its core, it embeds a global lifestyle into the Lingnan. Design-wise, under the concept of "Yunshan Green Scenery," it draws on Lingnan garden techniques and adopts a hybrid "block + mall" model with multi-level entrances, an open-plan layout, and interconnected rooftops, gardens, and ecological courtyards, enabling seamless transitions between shopping, sports, socializing, and nature. Ecologically, a green axis links a jogging park, a light outdoor base (rock climbing, camping, cycling), and a pet park, with regular events such as pet festivals. Culturally, the project collaborates with intangible cultural heritage inheritors and local artists to host the "Lingnan Culture Festival" and "Lingnan Cultural Heritage Bazaar," while also introducing international art festivals and youth gatherings to build a platform for cross-cultural exchange. In terms of business, it features an international membership supermarket, a five-star hotel, Grade-A office towers, and street-level retail, operating 24 hours a day. For consumption, over 600 brands offer dining options ranging from global cuisines to local specialties. The project champions "community co-creation," recruiting curator-style retail and community-focused brands, encouraging store managers to deeply operate their outlets, and regularly hosting brand crossover events, sustainable living discussions, as well as promoting eco-friendly materials, low-carbon operations, and organizing swap meets and environmental workshops. Baiyun District's "14th Five-Year Plan" includes Baiyun New Town in the "One Park, Two Cities, Three Hubs, Four Zones" major platform framework, aiming to build "a new highland of industry-city integration and a city living room." In this area, Baiyun Airport ranks among the nation's busiest in annual passenger throughput, Guangzhou Baiyun Railway Station is already operational, and the vicinity is home to consulates, international schools, and high-end residential communities, yet a lifestyle destination matching international traffic volume has been lacking. Yunshan CorePark answers this gap, blending Cantonese cultural heritage with a global perspective, and upon completion will elevate the region's commercial standing. About KINGBUY Commercial Headquartered in Guangzhou's Baiyun District and founded in 2004, KINGBUY Commercial specializes in the development and operation of mixed-use commercial complexes in urban cores. It has successfully operated 9 projects in Guangzhou and Foshan. Yunshan CorePark represents its highest-tier product line, aiming to redefine the regional lifestyle.

results.title: وداعاً للصلع.. اكتشافات علمية مذهلة تعيد رسم مستقبل علاج تساقط الشعر
results.description:
جملةً من الاكتشافات العلمية الحديثة قد تُعيد رسم خارطة علاج الصلع وتساقط الشعر، في مقدمتها اكتشاف توصّل إليه باحثون من جامعة شيفيلد البريطانية بالتعاون مع جامعة COMSATS الباكستانية، إذ تبيَّن أن سكراً طبيعياً يُعرف بـ"ديوكسي رايبوز" الداخل في تركيب الحمض النووي (DNA)، أعاد إنبات الشعر لدى فئران مصابة بالصلع الوراثي بسرعة ملحوظة.

وطوَّر باحثون كوريون من معهد KAIST جهازاً قابلاً للارتداء على شكل قبعة ذكية، يعتمد تقنية ضوئية متقدمة أثبتت قدرتها على إبطاء شيخوخة بصيلات الشعر بنسبة 92%، فيما كشف فريق تايواني عن مصل يعتمد الأحماض الدهنية لتنشيط الخلايا الجذعية الخاملة وإعادة إنتاج بصيلات الشعر في غضون 20 يوماً، بحسب ما ذكره موقع العربية.


علاجات غير جراحية


وعلى صعيد آخر، حدّد فريق من العلماء الأنظمة الجزيئية التي تتحكم في نمو شعر الإنسان، مما يفتح آفاقاً جديدة للعلاجات الطبيعية غير الجراحية، وهو ما اعتُبر اكتشافاً رائداً يسلط الضوء على الدور الحاسم للخلايا الجذعية وبروتينات الإشارة في تنشيط بصيلات الشعر وتجديدها.

ويرى الباحثون أن فهم هذه الأنظمة قد يُمهّد لجيل جديد من العلاجات الموجّهة التي تتجاوز حدود الأدوية التقليدية.

وتأتي هذه الاكتشافات في سياق تصاعد الطلب على بدائل أكثر أماناً لعلاج الصلع الوراثي، في ظل مخاوف متزايدة من الآثار الجانبية للعلاجات الدوائية الشائعة كـ"فيناسترايد" و"مينوكسيديل"، مما دفع كثيرين للبحث عن خيارات طبيعية أو أقل حدة. وتمثل هذه الاكتشافات مجتمعةً منعطفاً علمياً واعداً نحو علاجات أكثر فاعلية وأماناً.

results.title: Lucas Chancel’s powerful argument: Energy is politics
results.description: Lucas Chancel’s powerful argument: Energy is politics The book’s audience are academics but the general reader will find much food for thought in its four hundred plus pages of tightly-argued research. Wednesday 01/07/2026 Cover of “Energy and Inequalities, a Political History,” by Lucas Chancel, Le Seuil, 2025. The key point made by Lucas Chancel, whose research on inequality and climate issues is widely recognised is that energy issues should be seen not only as resources or technological issues, but also as political, social, and economic issues. Historically Chancel and a number of his peers have demonstrated that inequality and energy issues have been closely intertwined but listening to debates in the French media during the recent heatwave to hit Paris where I live made the arguments advanced by the author even more compelling. The broader public in France, Europe and beyond are finally grasping a key point which needs to find a political translation if we really want to address the climate challenge we face seriously. The author is a professor of economics at Sciences-Po Paris and co-director with Thomas Piketty of the World Inequality Lab at the Paris School of Economics (PSE). The book’s audience are academics but the general reader will find much food for thought in its four hundred plus pages of tightly-argued research. The graphs, excellent by any standards are well-integrated into a text that the range of topics covered is very broad and include an illuminating history of energy use since the Neolithic era. The final chapters look at the post-fossil world which the author argues will be characterised by the same energy-inequality as the past unless governments the right choices which he believes must meet the social demands for equity and justice which characterised the 20th century, at least till the Thatcher-Reagan revolution in the Anglo-Saxon countries. The author illustrates the often forgotten fact that “history is full of options and combinations ... past regimes have been diverse, including past fossil fuel regimes Chapter 6). Britain’s privatisation of its coal sector in the 1970s and 1980s, Norway’s production of hydrocarbons for export rather than domestic consumption, and France’s continued public control of some energy companies in the late twentieth century are examples of divergences that show how political choices matter when it comes to energy supply and distribution.” (Antione Missemer : Lucas Chancel, “Energies et Inegalites: une histoire politique”, OEconomia, 16 January 2026, p173-179). It is also worth remembering that politics, in the past, have taken precedence over financial and economic considerations. “After World War II, British public debt was already dramatically high when the government decided to spend money on nationalising the energy sector.” (idem). Financial obstacles should not stand in the way to the expression of democratic will. What was true yesterday must remain true tomorrow. A book such as this inevitably risks energy determinism. Chapters 2 and 3 suggest Chancel is aware of this risk but he insists in his conclusion that “energy has always shaped social inequalities.” If that is not determinism, I do not know what is. The politisation of ecology in the energy transition is addressed briefly at the end of the book and the author seems to side with those who argue that it should, so that “political struggle can contribute to advancing the tradition. This is a respectable position. But when we know that 4-60 percent of voters in democracies vote for right wing parties in elections and since some of them would not vote for left wing parties anyway, is it really a guarantee of success to politicise ecology? Is there a danger of framing ecological imperatives an essentially left wing, at the risk of losing right wing voters?”(idem).I think there is already a risk and nowhere is it more evident than in France. Pushing the transition against a part of the electorate that would not recognise itself in a policy that would “be seen as only left wing” hardly seems wise to this observer of the French political scene. Whatever questions one may raise about this book, one of its great strengths is that it devotes chapters to non-Western countries, particular to India and China, whose industrial strategy for energy transition is examined in detail. This reader would have welcomed more analysis on China’s policies in Africa but that will have to wait for another book. Professor Chancel teaches at the PSE which was founded in 2006 and is presided by Esther Dufflo, a French Nobel laureate in economics who splits her time between Paris and the MIT in Boston. The PSE includes Gabriel Zucman who is leading the drive to impose a global wealth tax on the super-rich and Thomas Piketty whose “Capital in the 20th Century” was an international bestseller. Its central theme was that because he rate of capital return in developed countries is persistently greater than the rate of economic growth, this will cause growing wealth inequality in the future. That of course is one of the key reasons for the rise in populist policies. Lucas Chancel is in good company with what one observer described as “the urgent economists.” (Meet the insurgent economists promoting a wealth tax, Simon Kuper, FT 21 May 20925). What is not is doubt is that Chancel’s latest book deserves to be widely read in both oil and gas producing and consuming countries and that the economists who teach at the PSE add to the reputation of the university of Paris.
creator: francis ghiles
